title: OAuth, Secrets, Identity, and Least Privilege
description: Secure a remote MCP server serving user-specific data. Learners cumulatively produce an OAuth and credential-handling design through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Decide when authentication and user authorization are required by data and action scope.
Implement protected resource metadata and OAuth-aligned authorization for remote HTTP.
Validate token audience, issuer, scopes, expiry, and resource binding.
